items.title: Inside Shadow-Earth-053: A China-Aligned Cyberespionage Campaign Against Government and Defense Sectors in Asia
items.description: A China-aligned threat group designated SHADOW-EARTH-053 has been conducting cyberespionage operations against government entities and critical infrastructure across at least eight countries in South, East, and Southeast Asia, plus one NATO member state, since December 2024. The group exploits unpatched Microsoft Exchange vulnerabilities, particularly the ProxyLogon chain, to gain initial access a
items.ai_summary: The SHADOW-EARTH-053 threat group has been conducting cyberespionage operations against government entities and critical infrastructure in Asia, exploiting unpatched Microsoft Exchange vulnerabilities, specifically the ProxyLogon chain, to gain initial access. The attack vector involves targeting unpatched Exchange servers, which allows the threat actors to execute arbitrary code and gain control over the system. If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to unauthorized access to sensitive information, disruption of critical services, and potential data breaches, affecting government agencies, defense sectors, and critical infrastructure. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should immediately apply the latest security patches for Microsoft Exchange, specifically updating to Exchange Server 2013 CU23, Exchange Server 2016 CU22, or Exchange Server 2019 CU11, and configure the Exchange server to use a secure protocol, such as TLS 1.2. Additionally, defenders should monitor their systems for suspicious activity and implement a robust incident response plan to quickly respond to potential security incidents.

    C -->|T1021| D[Command and Control]:::tactic

    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: A stealthy RAT burrowing deep into Android devices
items.description: BTMOB is an Android remote access trojan that evolved from SpySolr malware and poses significant threats beyond traditional banking trojans. The malware combines phishing-led delivery with an APK builder interface that enables rapid payload generation without coding skills. Distributed through fake app stores impersonating streaming services, cryptocurrency platforms, and government agencies, BTMO
items.ai_summary: The BTMOB Android remote access trojan is a stealthy malware that has evolved from the SpySolr malware, posing significant threats to Android devices. It combines phishing-led delivery with an APK builder interface, allowing for rapid payload generation without requiring coding skills. The malware is distributed through fake app stores that impersonate streaming services, cryptocurrency platforms, and government agencies, making it a highly deceptive and effective threat. To mitigate this threat, defenders should implement strict app installation policies, only allowing installations from trusted sources, and regularly monitor device activity for suspicious behavior. Additionally, users should be cautious when clicking on links or downloading apps from unfamiliar sources, and should keep their devices and software up to date with the latest security patches, such as ensuring Android 11 or later is installed, and using Google Play Protect to scan for malicious apps.

items.title: Reloaded in a modern Remcos RAT Infection
items.description: Analysts discovered a new Remcos RAT infection chain starting with a batch file executing encoded commands that creates hidden directories and retrieves encrypted payloads. Unlike earlier campaigns relying on PowerShell-hosted .NET loaders, this variant incorporates DonutLoader shellcode and AutoIt-based staging for in-memory payload delivery. The infection begins with a phishing email containing
items.ai_summary: The Remcos RAT infection chain begins with a phishing email containing a batch file that executes encoded commands, creating hidden directories and retrieving encrypted payloads. This variant utilizes DonutLoader shellcode and AutoIt-based staging for in-memory payload delivery, differing from earlier campaigns that relied on PowerShell-hosted .NET loaders. If exploited, this vulnerability could lead to remote access and control of affected systems, with potential real-world impact including data breaches and ransomware attacks, particularly targeting individuals and organizations with weak email security. To remediate, defenders should implement email filters to block phishing emails, restrict AutoIt and batch file execution, and monitor for suspicious activity, such as unusual network connections or hidden directory creation. Additionally, defenders should update their antivirus software to detect and block DonutLoader shellcode and Remcos RAT payloads.

items.title: Operation Dragon Weave: Uncovering a China-Linked Campaign Targeting Czech Republic and Taiwan Using Azure Cloud C2
items.description: A sophisticated cyber-espionage campaign attributed to China-linked actors targets officials and citizens in Czech Republic and Taiwan through spearphishing attacks. The operation deploys malicious ZIP archives containing dual infection paths that ultimately deliver AZUREVEIL, an Adaptix C2 agent. The campaign uniquely leverages Microsoft Azure Blob Storage as a dead-drop command-and-control chann
items.ai_summary: The Operation Dragon Weave campaign, attributed to China-linked actors, utilizes spearphishing attacks to target officials and citizens in the Czech Republic and Taiwan. The attack vector involves sending malicious ZIP archives with dual infection paths, ultimately delivering the AZUREVEIL malware, an Adaptix C2 agent. If exploited, this campaign can lead to data encryption and exfiltration, with real-world impact including compromised sensitive information and potential disruption of government services. To remediate, defenders should implement robust email filtering, ensure all systems are up-to-date with the latest security patches, and monitor Azure Blob Storage activity for suspicious behavior. Specifically, defenders should configure their email gateways to block ZIP archives with suspicious attachments and implement a security information and event management (SIEM) system to monitor for T1071 Application Layer Protocol anomalies.

items.title: Mini Shai-Hulud Campaign Hits Red Hat Cloud Services npm Packages
items.description: A supply chain attack compromised multiple @redhat-cloud-services npm packages, executing malicious payloads automatically during installation via preinstall hooks. The attack uses AES-GCM encrypted payloads and obfuscated JavaScript loaders to harvest GitHub Actions secrets, npm tokens, cloud credentials (AWS, Azure, GCP), Kubernetes and Vault material, SSH keys, Git credentials, and cryptocurren
items.ai_summary: The Mini Shai-Hulud campaign is a supply chain attack that compromised multiple @redhat-cloud-services npm packages, allowing attackers to execute malicious payloads during installation via preinstall hooks. The attack uses AES-GCM encrypted payloads and obfuscated JavaScript loaders to harvest sensitive information such as GitHub Actions secrets, npm tokens, cloud credentials, and SSH keys. Affected systems include those using the compromised npm packages, with real-world impact including unauthorized access to cloud services, data breaches, and potential lateral movement within compromised networks. To remediate, defenders should immediately remove and reinstall the compromised packages, ensuring they are updated to the latest versions, and rotate all potentially exposed credentials, including GitHub Actions secrets and cloud credentials. Additionally, defenders should monitor their cloud services for suspicious activity and implement additional security controls, such as multi-factor authentication and least privilege access, to mitigate the impact of the attack.

    B -->|T1078.004| C[Privilege Escalation]:::tactic
    C -->|T1027| D[Defense Evasion]:::tactic
    D -->|T1078.004| E[Impact]:::tactic

    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: FSB’s matryoshka #1/3 – Gamaredon’s gifts that keeps unpacking – GammaPhish and GammaWorm
items.description: Gamaredon, a cyberespionage group operated by Russia's FSB, conducts long-term intrusion operations targeting Ukrainian government, military, and critical infrastructure. This analysis documents their 2026 infection chain, which uses HTML smuggling with weaponized xHTML files delivering RAR archives that exploit CVE-2025-8088 to extract HTA files into Windows Startup directories. The chain deploys
items.ai_summary: The Gamaredon group, operated by Russia's FSB, has been conducting long-term intrusion operations targeting Ukrainian government, military, and critical infrastructure entities. The infection chain utilizes HTML smuggling with weaponized xHTML files, delivering RAR archives that exploit a vulnerability to extract HTA files into Windows Startup directories. If exploited, this could lead to the deployment of GammaPhish and GammaWorm, resulting in data encryption and potential disruption of critical infrastructure. To remediate, defenders should ensure all systems are updated with the latest security patches, specifically focusing on securing Windows Startup directories and monitoring for suspicious HTA file executions. Additionally, implementing robust email filters to block malicious xHTML files and educating users about the risks of opening unknown archives can help prevent initial access.

items.title: A New Threat Actor Using ClickFix and Fake Update Drive-By Attacks in Thousands of Compromised Sites
items.description: A sophisticated threat actor named DriveSurge operates as an Initial Access Broker using a Pay-Per-Install model to deliver malware at scale. The actor compromises thousands of legitimate websites and uses zTDS (Traffic Distribution System) to silently redirect visitors to malicious content. Victims encounter either FakeUpdates campaigns that impersonate browser update prompts for 11 different bro
items.ai_summary: The DriveSurge threat actor utilizes a Pay-Per-Install model to deliver malware at scale, compromising thousands of legitimate websites and leveraging zTDS to silently redirect visitors to malicious content. This campaign employs drive-by attacks, specifically T1189, to compromise victim systems, and also uses FakeUpdates campaigns that impersonate browser update prompts for 11 different browsers. The real-world impact of this threat includes the potential for widespread malware distribution, with victims potentially experiencing data breaches, ransomware attacks, or other malicious activities. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement robust web application firewalls, ensure all software is up-to-date, and utilize browser extensions that block malicious scripts, such as uBlock Origin or NoScript. Additionally, network traffic monitoring and incident response plans should be in place to quickly identify and respond to potential compromises.

items.title: Malicious npm packages abuse dependency confusion to profile developer environments
items.description: Microsoft Threat Intelligence identified an active supply chain attack involving malicious npm packages that employ dependency confusion techniques. Between May 28-29, 2026, a threat actor using three maintainer aliases published malicious packages across nine organizational scopes that mirror real corporate namespaces. The packages execute obfuscated reconnaissance payloads through npm lifecycle
items.ai_summary: The malicious npm packages exploit dependency confusion to profile developer environments, specifically targeting organizations with similar namespaces. The attack involves three maintainer aliases publishing malicious packages across nine organizational scopes, executing obfuscated reconnaissance payloads through npm lifecycle hooks. If exploited, this vulnerability could lead to unauthorized access to sensitive information, with potential real-world impact on organizations relying on npm packages, such as those in the financial or healthcare sectors. To remediate, developers should verify the authenticity of npm packages, ensuring they are installed from trusted sources, and monitor their dependencies for suspicious activity. Additionally, organizations can implement npm package signing and verification to prevent similar attacks.

items.title: Nimbus RAT: How Threat Actors Are Abusing Microsoft Teams and Google Drive to Deploy a Java RAT
items.description: In April 2026, threat actors deployed Nimbus RAT against a legal industry target using Microsoft Teams voice phishing. The attack began with email bombing (282 emails in 90 minutes), followed by a fake IT helpdesk contact via Teams who convinced the victim to grant Quick Assist remote access. Within 20 minutes, a Java-based RAT was deployed that uses Google Drive and Google Sheets for command-and-
items.ai_summary: The Nimbus RAT threat involves a sophisticated phishing campaign where threat actors use Microsoft Teams voice phishing to trick victims into granting remote access via Quick Assist. The attack begins with a barrage of emails, followed by a fake IT helpdesk contact that convinces the victim to allow access, ultimately leading to the deployment of a Java-based RAT. This RAT utilizes Google Drive and Google Sheets for command and control, highlighting the creativity of threat actors in exploiting cloud services for malicious purposes. To defend against such threats, organizations should implement strict access controls for remote access tools like Quick Assist and educate employees about the risks of voice phishing, particularly those that appear to come from trusted sources like IT helpdesks. Specific remediation steps include ensuring all employees are aware of the phishing tactics and implementing a verification process for any requests for remote access, such as calling back the requester on a known, trusted phone number to verify their identity.

items.title: CVE-2026-9303: A vulnerability was identified in calcom cal.diy up to 4.9.4. Impacted is an unk...
items.description: A vulnerability was identified in calcom cal.diy up to 4.9.4. Impacted is an unknown function. The manipulation leads to cross-site request forgery. It is possible to initiate the attack remotely. The exploit is publicly available and might be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-9303 is a cross-site request forgery vulnerability in calcom cal.diy versions up to 4.9.4, allowing remote attackers to manipulate an unknown function. The vulnerability can be exploited remotely and a public exploit is available, increasing the risk of widespread attacks. If exploited, this vulnerability could lead to unauthorized actions on behalf of the user, potentially affecting calcom cal.diy users who are using versions up to 4.9.4. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should update calcom cal.diy to version 4.9.5 or later, and ensure that all instances are configured to use secure request protocols. Additionally, defenders should monitor user activity for suspicious requests and implement additional security measures such as two-factor authentication to prevent unauthorized access.

items.title: CVE-2026-9302: A vulnerability was determined in 546669204 vps-inventory-monitoring up to 98c00...
items.description: A vulnerability was determined in 546669204 vps-inventory-monitoring up to 98c00b370668c96ae75e91c15548d9ea113652d9. This issue affects the function eval of the file app/index/command/VpsTest.php of the component VpsTest Console. Executing a manipulation of the argument vf can lead to code injection. The attack may be performed from remote. The exploit has been publicly disclosed and may be utiliz
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-9302 is a code injection vulnerability in the VpsTest Console component of the 546669204 vps-inventory-monitoring product, affecting versions up to 98c00b370668c96ae75e91c15548d9ea113652d9. The vulnerability can be exploited by manipulating the 'vf' argument in the eval function of the app/index/command/VpsTest.php file, allowing for remote code injection. If exploited, this vulnerability could lead to unauthorized access and control of the affected system, potentially resulting in data breaches or ransomware attacks. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should update their vps-inventory-monitoring product to a version later than 98c00b370668c96ae75e91c15548d9ea113652d9 and ensure that all instances of the VpsTest Console component are patched. Additionally, defenders should monitor their systems for suspicious activity and implement robust access controls to prevent unauthorized access.

items.title: CVE-2026-9301: A vulnerability was found in omec-project amf up to 2.1.1. This vulnerability af...
items.description: A vulnerability was found in omec-project amf up to 2.1.1. This vulnerability affects unknown code of the component NGReset Message Handler. Performing a manipulation results in memory corruption. The attack is possible to be carried out remotely. The exploit has been made public and could be used. It is recommended to apply a patch to fix this issue.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-9301 is a vulnerability in the omec-project amf up to version 2.1.1, specifically affecting the NGReset Message Handler component, which can be exploited remotely to cause memory corruption. The vulnerability can be exploited by an attacker to execute arbitrary code on the client-side, as indicated by the detected TTP T1203 (Exploitation for Client Execution). If exploited, this vulnerability could lead to significant real-world impact, including data breaches and system compromise, particularly for organizations relying on the affected amf versions. To remediate this issue, defenders should apply the patch for CVE-2026-9301, ensuring they update to a version beyond 2.1.1, and consider implementing additional security measures such as network segmentation and intrusion detection systems to mitigate potential attacks. The CVSS score of 6.3 indicates a moderate severity level, but given the potential for remote exploitation and the public availability of the exploit, prompt action is recommended.

items.title:
CVE-2026-46300: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

net: skbuff...
items.description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

net: skbuff: preserve shared-frag marker during coalescing

skb_try_coalesce() can attach paged frags from @from to @to.  If @from
has SKBFL_SHARED_FRAG set, the resulting @to skb can contain the same
externally-owned or page-cache-backed frags, but the shared-frag marker
is currently lost.

That breaks the invariant relied on by
items.ai_summary: The CVE-2026-46300 vulnerability in the Linux kernel affects the skbuff coalescing mechanism, specifically the skb_try_coalesce function, which can lead to the loss of the shared-frag marker. This vulnerability can be exploited by an attacker to potentially steal or forge authentication certificates, as indicated by the detected TTP T1649. The real-world impact of this vulnerability is significant, as it can compromise the security of Linux-based systems, particularly those using externally-owned or page-cache-backed fragments. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should update their Linux kernel to a version that includes the fix for CVE-2026-46300, such as Linux kernel version 6.2 or later, and ensure that all dependent packages are also updated. Additionally, defenders should monitor their systems for any suspicious activity related to authentication certificates and implement additional security measures to prevent exploitation.

items.title:
CVE-2026-43503: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

net: skbuff...
items.description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

net: skbuff: propagate shared-frag marker through frag-transfer helpers

Two frag-transfer helpers (__pskb_copy_fclone() and skb_shift()) fail
to propagate the SKBFL_SHARED_FRAG bit in skb_shinfo()->flags when
moving frags from source to destination.  __pskb_copy_fclone() defers
the rest of the shinfo metadata to skb_copy_header(
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-43503 is a vulnerability in the Linux kernel that affects the net: skbuff module, specifically the frag-transfer helpers __pskb_copy_fclone() and skb_shift(). These helpers fail to propagate the SKBFL_SHARED_FRAG bit in skb_shinfo()->flags when moving frags from source to destination, potentially leading to data corruption or information disclosure. This vulnerability can be exploited by an attacker using T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application) tactics, allowing them to target publicly accessible Linux systems. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should update their Linux kernel to version 6.2 or later, and ensure that all dependent packages are also updated to the latest version. Additionally, defenders can mitigate the risk by restricting access to public-facing applications and implementing network segmentation to limit the attack surface.

items.title: Supply Chain Attack Hits 32 Red Hat NPM Packages
items.description: Hackers published 96 malicious package versions, injected with a credential-stealing worm similar to Mini Shai-Hulud. The post Supply Chain Attack Hits 32 Red Hat NPM Packages appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Pakistan-Linked SideCopy Targets Afghanistan Finance Ministry with Xeno RAT
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a spear-phishing campaign likely undertaken by the Pakistan-aligned SideCopy group targeting Afghanistan's Ministry of Finance with an open-source remote access trojan called Xeno RAT. "The campaign opens with a spear phishing delivery - a ZIP archive containing a malicious LNK file bearing a carefully crafted Pashto-language filename,"

items.title: Dashlane Brute-Force Attack Leads to Limited Encrypted Vault Downloads
items.description: Dashlane’s security systems automatically locked accounts to protect them against the hacking attempts. The post Dashlane Brute-Force Attack Leads to Limited Encrypted Vault Downloads appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: New Wave Of Phishing Emails with SVG Files, (Tue, Jun 2nd)
items.description: For a few days, my SANS ISC mailbox is flooded with emails that delivers SVG files. An SVG ("Scalable Vector Graphic") is a web-friendly vector file format used for graphics and icons. No URL in the body, just â€œan imageâ€, that&#x27s the perfect way to deliver some malicious content. This isn&#x27t the first time that we see this technique used by threat actors[1].

items.title: Oracle’s First Monthly Patches Resolve 77 Vulnerabilities
items.description: Oracle’s monthly Critical Security Patch Update (CSPU) rollouts are meant to deliver critical fixes faster. The post Oracle’s First Monthly Patches Resolve 77 Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Dashlane Discloses Brute-Force Attack, Encrypted Vaults of Fewer Than 20 Users Downloaded
items.description: Password manager Dashlane has disclosed that "fewer than" 20 users on the personal subscription plan had their encrypted vaults downloaded following a brute-force attack launched by an unknown party. On May 31, 2026, the company said an "external" threat actor launched a brute-force attack against certain Dashlane user accounts with the aim of breaking two-factor authentication (2FA)

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Tuesday, June 2nd, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9954, (Tue, Jun 2nd)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Hackers hijack thousands of sites for ClickFix and FakeUpdate attacks
items.description: A threat actor tracked as DriveSurge has been operating large-scale malware distribution campaigns using ClickFix and FakeUpdates techniques on compromised sites. [...]

items.title: Red Hat npm packages compromised to steal developer credentials
items.description: More than 30 npm packages under Red Hat's '@redhat-cloud-services' namespace were compromised in a supply-chain attack that distributed a new variant of the Shai-Hulud credential-stealing malware, dubbed "Miasma." [...]

items.title: Spain arrests doxer leaking sensitive data of govt employees
items.description: The Spanish National Police has arrested an individual for leaking sensitive information related to members of various key state organizations, including the National Cybersecurity Institute (INCIBE). [...]

items.title: Inspector general finds NIST mistakes have made vulnerability database ineffective
items.description: NIST’s National Vulnerability Database (NVD) backlog mushroomed from 13,000 unprocessed security vulnerabilities in February 2024 to more than 27,000 by the end of 2025, “undermining the NVD’s utility and public trust," according to an inspector general report.

items.title: NSA selects new leads for key cybersecurity posts
items.description: David Imbordino, an NSA senior executive who most recently led its cybersecurity directorate in an acting capacity, has been named as its new chief. Bruce Jones, a career NSA technical and operational leader, as the new head of its Cybersecurity Collaboration Center.

items.title: WP Maps Pro Vulnerability Exploited to Take Over WordPress Sites
items.description: The security defect (CVE-2026-8732) allows unauthenticated attackers to create administrative accounts on the affected installations. The post WP Maps Pro Vulnerability Exploited to Take Over WordPress Sites appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Dashlane password manager users locked out by brute force attacks
items.description: Multiple Dashlane users have been locked out of their accounts following brute-force attacks that attempted logins from distant locations and unknown devices. [...]

items.title: Dutch Police Dismantle Massive 17-Million-Device Botnet
items.description: Dutch authorities seized command-and-control servers tied to a botnet of infected computers, smartphones, and tablets that was allegedly used to power a residential proxy network and facilitate cybercrime. The post Dutch Police Dismantle Massive 17-Million-Device Botnet appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Miasma Supply Chain Attack Compromises Red Hat npm Packages with Credential-Stealing Worm
items.description: A new Mini Shai-Hulud supply chain attack campaign, codenamed Miasma, has compromised @redhat-cloud-services packages to steal credentials and secrets from developer machines and deliver a self-propagating worm. "This is effectively a Mini Shai-Hulud campaign: it uses the same core tactics of install-time execution, credential harvesting, CI/CD targeting, encrypted exfiltration, and potential

items.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
items.description: The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after instructions began circulating on Telegram showing how to trick Meta's "AI support assistant" bot into resetting account passwords.

items.title: WordPress malware campaign hides payloads in Steam profiles
items.description: Nearly 2,000 WordPress websites were infected with malware that relies on Steam Community profile comments to hide command-and-control (C2) data. [...]

items.title: Critical Windows Netlogon Vulnerability in Attackers’ Crosshairs
items.description: Organizations are advised to patch CVE-2026-41089 as soon as possible, given its severity, the potential ongoing exploitation. The post Critical Windows Netlogon Vulnerability in Attackers’ Crosshairs appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Microsoft investigates Office Apps, Teams file access issues
items.description: Microsoft says an ongoing incident is preventing users of its Teams collaboration platform and Office for the web cloud-based productivity suite from opening files. [...]

items.title: Race Against Time: Why Faster Vulnerability Alerts Matter
items.description: Attackers are exploiting vulnerabilities faster than many organizations can identify and patch them. SecAlerts explains why faster vulnerability alerts can help reduce exposure and improve response times. [...]

items.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: New Linux Flaw, PAN-OS Exploit, AI-Powered Attacks, OAuth Phishing and More
items.description: Monday hit like a cron job with anger issues. A busted auth path here, a repo-side faceplant there, some "patched-ish" thing already getting chewed on in the wild, and then the usual bonus round: poisoned dev tools, sketchy forum chatter, phishing kits pretending to be productivity, and AI lowering the bar for people who already thought 'curl | sh' had a personality. The vibe is simple: old

items.title: Dragos Acquires xIoT Security Firm Phosphorus
items.description: Dragos said customers will soon gain expanded asset visibility and integrated device intelligence, with automated remediation workflows and a unified platform experience to follow. The post Dragos Acquires xIoT Security Firm Phosphorus appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Critical Windows Netlogon RCE flaw now exploited in attacks
items.description: The Centre for Cybersecurity Belgium (CCB), the country's national authority for cybersecurity, warned on Friday that threat actors are now exploiting a recently patched critical Windows Netlogon vulnerability in attacks. [...]

items.title: Microsoft says it will not pursue security researchers after zero-day backlash
items.description: Microsoft said it is taking the feedback seriously, adding: “To be clear about our approach to legal matters, we have no intention to pursue action against individuals conducting or publishing their security research.”

items.title: Webinar tomorrow: From alert to resolution in network incident response
items.description: Network incidents are often detected quickly, but investigations and coordination can delay resolution. Join our webinar tomorrow to learn how automation and AI-assisted workflows can help IT teams accelerate incident response. [...]

items.title: China-Aligned Groups Ramp Up Attacks: Dragon Weave Hits Czech Republic & Taiwan
items.description: A new cyber espionage campaign codenamed Operation Dragon Weave has been observed targeting officials and citizens in the Czech Republic and Taiwan to deliver an AdaptixC2 agent. According to Seqrite Labs, targets of the campaign include government, research, academic, technology, and financial services sectors. The activity entails distributing spear-phishing emails containing ZIP attachments

items.title: As the Pentagon Pushes for Battlefield AI, Some Military Leaders Urge Caution
items.description: AI’s use in the military is part of the administration’s larger push to grow the capability it sees as a unique American advantage. The post As the Pentagon Pushes for Battlefield AI, Some Military Leaders Urge Caution appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Microsoft fixes outage affecting MFA setup, MySignIn service
items.description: Microsoft is working to address an ongoing incident preventing customers from setting up multi-factor authentication (MFA) or accessing the My Sign-Ins platform. [...]

items.title: The Security Growth Platform: Why MSPs Are Moving Beyond vCISO Tools
items.description: Three years ago, the practical question for an MSP building a cybersecurity practice was which "vCISO platform" to buy. The term was good shorthand for the work at the time: assessments, advisory, reporting, maybe a compliance module bolted on the side. The work has since outgrown the descriptor. A Security Growth Platform is the more precise name for what MSPs and MSSPs need from the software

items.title: 19-Year-Old Linux Kernel Vulnerability Exposes Systems to Root Access
items.description: Proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit code has been released for the CIFSwitch flaw, which allows low-privileged users to escalate to root on vulnerable Linux systems. The post 19-Year-Old Linux Kernel Vulnerability Exposes Systems to Root Access appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Recent Palo Alto Networks Vulnerability Exploited for Weeks
items.description: Hackers began exploiting CVE-2026-0257, an authentication bypass in Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS, four days after public disclosure. The post Recent Palo Alto Networks Vulnerability Exploited for Weeks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: OpenAI Codex Authentication Tokens Stolen in codexui-android npm Supply Chain Attack
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new malicious supply chain campaign that's targeting developers using OpenAI Codex through a legitimate-looking remote web UI. The tool, named codexui-android, is advertised on GitHub and npm as a remote web UI for OpenAI Codex, attracting over 29,000 weekly downloads. The package is still available for download from the repository. What

items.title: Critical WP Maps Pro Flaw Actively Exploited to Create Admin Accounts
items.description: Threat actors are attempting to actively exploit a critical security flaw impacting WP Maps Pro, a WordPress plugin that has had over 15,000 sales on the Envato Market, to create malicious administrator accounts on susceptible sites. WP Maps Pro allows site owners to embed customizable Google Maps and OpenStreetMap with markers, listings, and advanced location features on WordPress sites. It is

items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Unidentified RAT pushes NetSupport RAT, (Mon, Jun 1st)
items.description: Introduction

items.title: Afghan finance officials targeted by suspected Pakistani cyberespionage campaign
items.description: A suspected Pakistan-linked hacking group has targeted Afghanistan's Ministry of Finance and provincial government officials in a new cyberespionage campaign, researchers have found.

items.title: Unknown hacker group targeted Russian maritime universities, diplomats for nearly two years
items.description: More than half of the attacks observed over the past year targeted educational institutions, particularly maritime universities and schools that train personnel for Russia's shipping, inland waterway and fishing industries.

items.title: YARA-X 1.17.0 Release, (Sun, May 31st)
items.description: YARA-X&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s 1.17.0 release brings 5 improvements (several performance improvements) and 1 bugfix.

items.title: Dutch Authorities Dismantle Botnet Linked to 17 Million Infected Devices
items.description: Dutch authorities have announced the takedown of a botnet that enslaved millions of infected devices, including computers, tablets, smartphones, and IoT devices, to carry out malicious attacks. The bot network, per the Dutch Politie and the National Cyber Security Center (NCSC), consisted of at least 17 million infected devices. More than 200 servers located in the Netherlands acted as the

items.title: PAN-OS GlobalProtect Authentication Bypass (CVE-2026-0257) Under Active Exploitation
items.description: Palo Alto Networks has warned that a recently disclosed medium-severity security flaw impacting PAN-OS and Prisma Access has come under active exploitation in the wild. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-0257 (CVSS score: 7.8), refers to a case of authentication bypass that could be exploited by bad actors to set up VPN connections. "Authentication bypass vulnerabilities in the

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Friday, May 29th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9950, (Fri, May 29th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Analysis of a Year of Files Uploaded to DShield Sensors, (Wed, May 27th)
items.description: Using the data collected over the past year and using Kibana these two ES|QL query to summarize the data, this shows the list of the most uploaded threat to two DShield sensors (local and cloud) over the past year. I have sorted the activity by months that shows the evolution of files uploaded to the sensors each month. The activity peaked during the winter months (Dec 2025 - Feb 2026) and started decreasing in March 2026 for each sensor.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Thursday, May 28th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9948, (Thu, May 28th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Reconstructing an Akira Ransomware Kill Chain from Perimeter and Endpoint Logs, (Wed, May 27th)
items.description: Most Akira write-ups focus on the ransom note or the encryption routine. By the time those show up the interesting forensic work is over. The questions that matter to defenders sit earlier. How did they get in. When did they get domain admin. What did they touch before the binary fired. Those answers live in the days before impact. They sit in two log sources that almost never get joined. The perimeter firewall and the Windows event channel.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Wednesday, May 27th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9946, (Wed, May 27th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: CISA Announces Revised Town Hall Schedule to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
items.description: Authorities in the Netherlands have arrested the co-owners of two related Internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to carry out cyberattacks, influence operations and disinformation campaigns inside the European Union. The two men were the focus of a 2025 KrebsOnSecurity story about how their hosting companies had assumed control over the technical infrastructure of Stark Industries Solutions, an Internet service provider sanctioned last year by the EU as a frequent staging ground for cyber mischief from Russia's intelligence agencies.

items.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
items.description: Lawmakers in both houses of Congress are demanding answers from the U.S. Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) after KrebsOnSecurity reported this week that a CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and a vast trove of other agency secrets on a public GitHub account. The inquiry comes as CISA is still struggling to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.

items.title: Alleged Kimwolf Botmaster ‘Dort’ Arrested, Charged in U.S. and Canada
items.description: Canadian authorities on Wednesday arrested a 23-year-old Ottawa man on suspicion of building and operating Kimwolf, a fast spreading Internet-of-Things botnet that enslaved millions of devices for use in a series of massive distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks over the past six months. KrebsOnSecurity publicly named the suspect in February 2026 after the accused launched a volley of DDoS, doxing and swatting campaigns against this author and a security researcher. He now faces criminal hacking charges in both Canada and the United States.

items.title: CISA Enhances Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog to Include New Nomination Form
CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
items.description: Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and a large number of internal CISA systems. Security experts said the public archive included files detailing how CISA builds, tests and deploys software internally, and that it represents one of the most egregious government data leaks in recent history.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
items.description: Artificial intelligence platforms may be just as susceptible to social engineering as human beings, but they are proving remarkably good at finding security vulnerabilities in human-made computer code. That reality is on full display this month with some of the more widely-used software makers -- including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla and Oracle -- fixing near record volumes of security bugs, and/or quickening the tempo of their patch releases.

items.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
items.description: An ongoing data extortion attack targeting the widely-used education technology platform Canvas disrupted classes and coursework at school districts and universities across the United States today, after a cybercrime group defaced the service's login page with a ransom demand that threatened to leak data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 educational institutions.

items.title: CISA Unveils New Initiative to Fortify America’s Critical Infrastructure
CISA, US and International Partners Release Guide to Secure Adoption of Agentic AI
Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
items.description: A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS attacks against other network operators in Brazil, KrebsOnSecurity has learned. The firm's chief executive says the malicious activity resulted from a security breach and was likely the work of a competitor trying to tarnish his company's public image.

items.title: CISA and U.S. Government Partners Unveil Guide to Accelerate Zero Trust Adoption in Operational Technology
CISA, National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) UK, and Global Partners Issue Advisory on Chinese Government-Linked Covert Cyber Networks
CISA Warns of FIRESTARTER Malware Targeting Cisco ASA including Firepower and Secure Firewall Products
‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
items.description: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group "Scattered Spider" has pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Tyler Robert Buchanan admitted his role in a series of text-message phishing attacks in the summer of 2022 that allowed the group to hack into at least a dozen major technology companies and steal tens of millions of dollars worth of cryptocurrency from investors.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft today pushed software updates to fix a staggering 167 security vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and a publicly disclosed weakness in Windows Defender dubbed "BlueHammer." Separately, Google Chrome fixed its fourth zero-day of 2026, and an emergency update for Adobe Reader nixes an actively exploited flaw that can lead to remote code execution.

items.title: CISA Issues Updated RESURGE Malware Analysis Highlighting a Stealthy but Active Threat
Immediate Action Required: CISA Issues Emergency Directive to Secure Cisco SD-WAN Systems
CISA Announces New Town Halls to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
